Comparing options
What to look for in a roofing bid
Not all quotes are measuring the same thing. Here's how to read them side by side.
Red flags
Watch out for these
Lighter underlayment
15# felt vs. synthetic or PSU-30 — the biggest invisible cut in a cheap bid.
Subcontracted labor
Subs have no accountability to the company whose name is on the contract.
Vague scope of work
"Full replacement" with no material specs means they quote the minimum once on your roof.
Low-trust labor warranty
A warranty from a company that may not exist in 3 years isn't protection.
The cheapest bid rarely stays cheap — thin underlayment and subs show up as leaks within 5 years.
Green flags
What a solid bid looks like
Clear, extensive scope
Every material named and specced — underlayment, shingles, flashing, ridge. No surprises.
In-house, licensed & insured
Ask for the license number — a real company hands it over without hesitation.
Manufacturer-certified
Certification unlocks 50-year warranties vs. 25 for non-certified installs of the same shingle.
High-trust labor warranty
A company that stands behind their work long-term — we plan on being around.
A contractor who answers every question without hesitation has nothing to hide.
← Back
